Eli Lilly to acquire Adverum Biotechnologies to strengthen position in ocular gene therapy
data.description:
Eli Lilly announces the signing of a definitive agreement to acquire Adverum Biotechnologies, a clinical-stage company specializing in intravitreal gene therapies for eye diseases.

The transaction values Adverum at $3.56 per share in cash, plus a contingent value right (CVR) of up to $8.91, for a total potential value of $12.47 per share.

Adverum's lead compound, Ixo-vec, is intended to treat wet age-related macular degeneration (wet AMD) with a single administration and has received several fast-track designations (Fast Track, RMAT, PRIME).

Andrew Adams, vice president at Lilly, highlights the potential to "transform the treatment of AMD." Eli Lilly will provide up to $65 million in secured financing to support clinical trials prior to the closing of the transaction, which is expected in Q4 2025.

The transaction was unanimously approved by Adverum's board of directors.

Henan Mingtai Al.Industrial Co.,Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Henan Mingtai Al.Industrial Co.,Ltd. reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 25,873.55 million compared to CNY 23,655.38 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 25,873.55 million compared to CNY 23,655.38 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 1,404.36 million compared to CNY 1,411.27 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.12 compared to CNY 1.18 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.11 compared to CNY 1.18 a year ago.

Guangzhou Automobile Group Co., Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Guangzhou Automobile Group Co., Ltd. reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 66,271.79 million compared to CNY 74,040.14 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 66,928.66 million compared to CNY 74,740.86 million a year ago.

Net loss was CNY 4,311.84 million compared to net income of CNY 120.06 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.42 compared to basic earnings per share from continuing operations of CNY 0.01 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.42 compared to diluted earnings per share from continuing operations of CNY 0.01 a year ago.

data.title: TSX opens higher as US inflation data boosts rate-cut hopes
data.description:
(Reuters) - Canada's main stock index opened higher on Friday after a softer-than-expected U.S. inflation report strengthened bets for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, while investors appeared undeterred by emerging tensions between the U.S. and Canada.

At 9:30 a.m. ET (1330 GMT), Toronto's S&P/TSX composite index was up 0.4% at 30,294.01 points.

(Reporting by Ragini Mathur in Bengaluru; Editing by Shailesh Kuber)

US October Inflation Data Unlikely to be Released Due to Government Shutdown, Press Secretary Leavitt Says
The Week in Oil : U.S. Sanctions on Russia Trigger Price Rally
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker


Here is a look at what happened in oil markets in the week of Oct. 20-24 and what the focus will be in the days to come.


OVERVIEW: Oil prices stabilize after rallying on new U.S. sanctions on Russia's oil sector. Brent crude trades at around $66 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate is around $62 a barrel.

The additional sanctions mark a step change in President Trump's efforts to end the Russia-Ukraine war and target a key source of revenue for Russia's war efforts.

The sanctions could lead to a tighter oil market but questions remain over how China and India will respond.

The European Union has also confirmed a ban on imports of Russian liquefied natural gas from 2027, while placing additional sanctions on Russia's shadow fleet of oil tankers.


GEOPOLITICAL RISKS: The U.S. sanctions targeting Lukoil and Rosneft caught the market by surprise, causing Brent crude futures to jump 5% on Thursday on fears of disruption to global supply.

The Trump administration has become increasingly frustrated with Russia stalling negotiations to end the conflict in Ukraine. Trump announced the measures after the Kremlin rejected Washington's call for a cease-fire along the current front lines. Plans for a second summit between the U.S. and Russian leaders have been canceled.

The impact of the sanctions depends on how well they are enforced, how major customers of Russian oil, India and China, respond, and the extent to which Russia can circumvent the new measures, analysts say.

India, Turkey and China are the biggest importers of Russian oil and while seaborne cargoes to India and Turkey are most at risk, while Chinese imports could be more resilient, HSBC analysts said.


MACRO: The White House has confirmed that President Trump is scheduled to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next Thursday. The talks could offer a path to de-escalating tensions between the two countries which reignited after China placed curbs on exports of rare-earth metals.

U.S. consumer prices rose 3% in September from a year earlier, a larger increase than August's increase of 2.9%. Investors will be examining the report for clues on the direction of interest rates.


SUPPLY AND DEMAND: Trade tensions between the U.S. and China continue to raise worries about cooling demand growth for oil.

Fears about a supply glut also remain but the additional U.S. sanctions could see supplies tighten. However, previous sanctions have had little effect on Russian supply and the oil market has adjusted with shifts in flows, the HSBC analyst said.

The new sanctions, combined with the EU's nineteenth round of sanctions against Russia, could lead to net supply losses from Russia of several hundred thousand barrels a day but the impact is too early to fully quantify, they added.


NEXT WEEK: Investors will be looking for signs that U.S.-China trade tensions could de-escalate at next week's meeting. They will also be looking for signals that the protracted U.S. government shutdown could end.

Third-quarter earnings continue next week and market watchers will be looking for signals on the health of the U.S. economy.


Write to adam.whitaker@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0933ET

data.title: Braskem Approves $782.6 Million Investment to Expand Rio de Janeiro Plant
Wall St opens higher as cool inflation data fuels Fed rate cut bets
data.description:
(Reuters) - Wall Street opened on solid ground on Friday, as investors welcomed a cooler-than-expected inflation reading, while Intel's upbeat results offered a boost.

At 09:30 a.m., the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 221.66 points, or 0.47%, to 46,956.27. The S&P 500 gained 43.88 points, or 0.66%, to 6,782.32, and the Nasdaq Composite gained 200.07 points, or 0.87%, to 23,141.87.

(Reporting by Pranav Kashyap in Bangalore; Editing by Shilpi Majumdar)

US Core CPI Rises Less Than Expected in September
data.description:
Underlying US inflation rose by less than expected in September, increasing 0.2% from August, the slowest pace in three months. On an annual basis, it advanced 3%. Michael McKee breaks down the numbers on Bloomberg Television.
